session.user:
Find and read these files for the PlanService ABC, real, and fake: 1. Look for the PlanService ABC - search for files containing "class PlanService" under packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_service/ 2. The real implementation 3. The fake implementation
Report: full paths, the class definitions, and where I should add a new `fetch_run_jobs` method (the pattern for adding methods).
Also find the CheckRunsScreen (src/erk/tui/screens/check_runs_screen.py) and report its structure so I can use it as a pattern for creating RunDetailScreen.
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.
session.assistant: I'll help you find these files and understand the patterns. Let me start by checking the documentation index as instructed.
session.tool_result:
1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Agent Documentation 5→ 6→Before starting work, scan the read-when conditions below. 7→If your current task matches, read the linked document **before writing code**. 8→ 9→## Categories 10→ 11→- [architecture/](architecture/) — Explore when working on core patterns (dry-run, gateways, subprocess, shell integration). Add docs here for cross-cutting technical patterns. 12→- [capabilities/](capabilities/) 13→- [changelog/](changelog/) 14→- [checklists/](checklists/) 15→- [ci/](ci/) 16→- [claude-code/](claude-code/) 17→- [cli/](cli/) — Explore when building CLI commands or output formatting. Add docs here for Click patterns and terminal UX. 18→- [commands/](commands/) — Explore when creating or optimizing slash commands. Add docs here for command authoring patterns. 19→- [config/](config/) 20→- [configuration/](configuration/) 21→- [documentation/](documentation/) — Explore when structuring or writing documentation. Add docs here for documentation methodology and best practices. 22→- [erk/](erk/) — Explore when working with erk-specific workflows (worktrees, PR sync, Graphite). Add docs here for erk user-facing features. 23→- [erk-dev/](erk-dev/) 24→- [gateway/](gateway/) 25→- [hooks/](hooks/) — Explore when creating or debugging hooks. Add docs here for hook development patterns. 26→- [integrations/](integrations/) 27→- [objectives/](objectives/) 28→- [planning/](planning/) — Explore when working with plans, .erk/impl-context/ folders, or agent delegation. Add docs here for planning workflow patterns. 29→- [pr-operations/](pr-operations/) 30→- [refactoring/](refactoring/)
... [22 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: Documentation Guide 3→last_audited: "2026-02-17 16:00 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "navigating erk documentation" 7→ - "finding where documentation lives" 8→ - "understanding doc organization" 9→--- 10→ 11→# Documentation Guide 12→ 13→## Quick Navigation 14→ 15→The documentation is organized by topic category: 16→ 17→### Documentation Categories 18→ 19→| Category | Description | 20→| ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | 21→| [Architecture](architecture/) | Core patterns, interfaces, subprocess wrappers | 22→| [CLI Development](cli/) | Command organization, output styling, formatting | 23→| [Planning](planning/) | Plan lifecycle, workflows, agent delegation | 24→| [Testing](testing/) | Test architecture, fakes, rebase conflicts | 25→| [Sessions](sessions/) | Session logs, context analysis, tools | 26→| [Hooks](hooks/) | Hook system, erk-specific hooks | 27→| [Commands](commands/) | Slash command optimization | 28→| [Reference](reference/) | GitHub integration, external references | 29→| [Erk](erk/) | Erk-specific workflows | 30→

session.assistant: Good. Now let me search the relevant documentation categories. Since I'm looking for gateway patterns (PlanService ABC) and TUI patterns (CheckRunsScreen), I should check both architecture and TUI docs.
Let me read the most relevant architecture and TUI documentation files:
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: TUI Architecture Overview 3→read_when: 4→ - "understanding TUI structure" 5→ - "implementing TUI components" 6→ - "working with TUI data providers" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "reusing same DOM element id across loading/empty/content states" 9→ warning: "query_one() returns wrong element silently when id is reused across lifecycle phases. Use unique IDs per phase." 10→ - action: "calling widget methods directly from @work(thread=True) background threads" 11→ warning: "Direct widget calls from background threads cause silent UI corruption. Must use self.app.call_from_thread(callback, ...)." 12→ - action: "extending PlanDataProvider ABC" 13→ warning: "Requires 3-file update: abc.py + real.py + fake.py. Fake must initialize new dict in __init__. Missing init causes AttributeError at test time." 14→ - action: "adding a DataTable column with add_column(key=...)" 15→ warning: "Column key is a data binding contract — must match data field name. Silent failure when mismatched." 16→ - action: "moving @on decorated event handlers to a mixin" 17→ warning: "Textual's _MessagePumpMeta only scans class.__dict__, not inherited methods. Event handlers on mixins are silently ignored." 18→ - action: "adding CI check formatting to TUI screens" 19→ warning: "Use src/erk/tui/formatting/ci_checks.py shared module (format_check_line, format_summary_blockquote, format_check_runs) instead of duplicating formatting logic." 20→last_audited: "2026-02-17 18:30 PT" 21→audit_result: clean 22→--- 23→ 24→# TUI Architecture Overview 25→ 26→The erk TUI is built with Textual and follows a layered architecture separating data fetching, filtering, and rendering. 27→ 28→## Directory Structure 29→ 30→```
... [183 lines omitted] ...
128→Use fake providers instead of mocking. `FakePlanDataProvider` lives in `erk_shared.gateway.plan_data_provider.fake` and accepts keyword-only arguments (`plans`, `clipboard`, `browser`, `repo_root`, `fetch_error`). The `make_plan_row()` helper in the same module creates test `PlanRowData` instances with sensible defaults.

1→--- 2→title: TUI Modal Screen Pattern 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding a new modal screen to the TUI" 5→ - "implementing a ModalScreen subclass" 6→ - "displaying detail views or confirmation dialogs" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "creating a ModalScreen without CSS for dismiss behavior" 9→ warning: "ModalScreen requires explicit CSS for the overlay. Without it, clicking outside the modal does nothing." 10→ - action: "calling widget methods from @work(thread=True) without call_from_thread()" 11→ warning: "Background thread widget mutations cause silent UI corruption. Use self.app.call_from_thread(callback, ...)." 12→ - action: "using inverted key check in on_key() modal dismiss logic" 13→ warning: "if event.key not in (...) is WRONG for dismiss logic — it swallows dismiss keys. Use if event.key in (...) to check for positive dismiss. Regression caused by stacked PR merge order." 14→ score: 7 15→--- 16→ 17→# TUI Modal Screen Pattern 18→ 19→Checklist for implementing a new modal screen in the erk TUI. 20→ 21→## 7-Element Checklist 22→ 23→### 1. ModalScreen Subclass 24→ 25→Extend `ModalScreen` (not `Screen`) to get overlay dismiss behavior: 26→ 27→```python 28→from textual.screen import ModalScreen 29→ 30→class MyDetailScreen(ModalScreen):
... [101 lines omitted] ...
66→ except Exception as e: 67→ self.app.call_from_thread(self._on_error, str(e)) 80→### 6. Error Boundary 82→Wrap the entire worker body in try/except. This is an approved EAFP exception for UI error boundaries — it prevents silent worker failures. 125→- [Textual Async Best Practices](textual-async.md) — Error boundary pattern details

1→--- 2→title: Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist 3→last_audited: "2026-02-15 18:50 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "adding or modifying methods in any gateway ABC interface (Git, GitHub, Graphite)" 7→ - "implementing new gateway operations" 8→ - "composing one gateway inside another (e.g., GitHub composing GitHubIssues)" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "creating a new gateway ABC" 11→ warning: "Default is 3-file pattern (abc.py, real.py, fake.py). Only add dry_run.py if the gateway participates in a user-facing --dry-run feature. Most gateways do not." 12→ - action: "adding a new method to a dry-run-enabled gateway ABC (Git, LocalGitHub, Graphite)" 13→ warning: "Must implement in 4 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py." 14→ - action: "adding a new method to a 3-file gateway ABC" 15→ warning: "Must implement in 3 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py." 16→ - action: "removing an abstract method from a gateway ABC" 17→ warning: "Must remove from all implementation files simultaneously (3 or 4 depending on pattern). Partial removal causes type checker errors. Update all call sites. Verify with grep across packages." 18→ - action: "adding subprocess.run or run_subprocess_with_context calls to a gateway real.py file" 19→ warning: "Must add integration tests in tests/integration/test_real_*.py. Real gateway methods with subprocess calls need tests that verify the actual subprocess behavior." 20→ pattern: "subprocess\\.run\\(|run_subprocess_with_context\\(" 21→ - action: "making N sequential gh api calls in a loop when a single GraphQL query could fetch all data" 22→ warning: "Each gh subprocess call costs ~200-300ms overhead. Batch into a single GraphQL query with node fragments when fetching multiple items. See http-accelerated-plan-refresh.md for the dual-path pattern." 23→ - action: "using subprocess.run with git command outside of a gateway" 24→ warning: "Use the Git gateway instead. Direct subprocess calls bypass testability (fakes) and dry-run support. The Git ABC (erk_shared.gateway.git.abc.Git) likely already has a method for this operation. Only use subprocess directly in real.py gateway implementations." 25→ pattern: "subprocess\\.run\\(\\s*\\[.*[\"']git" 26→ - action: "changing gateway return type to discriminated union" 27→ warning: "Verify all implementations import the new types. For 4-file gateways: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py. For 3-file gateways: abc.py, real.py, fake.py." 28→ - action: "designing error handling for a new gateway method" 29→ warning: "Ask: does the caller continue after the failure? If yes, use discriminated union. If all callers terminate, use exceptions. See 'Non-Ideal State Decision Checklist' section." 30→ - action: "adding a new parameter to a gateway ABC method"
... [782 lines omitted] ...
31→ warning: "All implementations must be updated (3 or 4 depending on pattern). Fake may accept but not track new parameters when assertion is not needed for tests." 33→ warning: "Gateway names must reflect the TOOL being wrapped, not the execution mechanism. Use LocalGitHub for gh calls, Git for git calls, CmuxGateway for cmux calls, PromptExecutor for claude calls. A mechanism-named gateway is just moving the mock up one layer without gaining abstraction." 35→ warning: "Must also wire into ErkContext: add field to context.py dataclass, add parameter to for_test(), and wire Real* in production factory (src/erk/core/context.py). See 'New Gateway: ErkContext Wiring' section." 44→**Exception: erk-dev** (`packages/erk-dev/`) is developer tooling and is exempt from these rules. Direct `subprocess.run` with git commands is acceptable in erk-dev since it doesn't need gateway abstractions for its own operations. 172→When designing error handling for a new gateway method, the key question is: **does the caller continue after the failure?** 174→This checklist helps you choose between discriminated unions and exceptions. For full pattern documentation, see [Discriminated Union Error Handling](discriminated-union-error-handling.md). 176→### Decision: Discriminated Union or Exception? 180→| Does at least one caller branch on the error and continue with different logic? | Discriminated union | Exception | 181→| Does the error carry domain-meaningful fields beyond just `message`? | Discriminated union | Exception | 182→| Do all callers terminate on failure (extract message and stop)? | Exception | Discriminated union | 186→- [ ] At least one caller branches on the error and continues with different logic 187→- [ ] Error type carries domain-meaningful fields beyond just `message` 188→- [ ] Error type implements the `NonIdealState` protocol (`error_type` + `message` properties) from `erk_shared.non_ideal_state` 192→### Checklist: Use Exceptions When 194→- [ ] All callers terminate on failure (extract message and stop) 195→- [ ] No caller inspects error type or branches on error content 196→- [ ] Use `UserFacingCliError` at CLI boundary 202→All discriminated union error types must implement the `NonIdealState` protocol from `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/non_ideal_state.py`. The protocol requires two read-only properties: `error_type` (machine-readable classification string) and `message` (human-readable description). 217→**Methods using discriminated unions** (callers branch on error): 221→| `merge_pr` | `MergeResult \| MergeError` | GitHub | 222→| `push_to_remote` | `PushResult \| PushError` | Git remote_ops | 223→| `pull_rebase` | `PullRebaseResult \| PullRebaseError` | Git remote_ops | 225→| `submit_branch` | `SubmitBranchResult \| SubmitBranchError` | BranchManager | 227→**Methods using exceptions** (all callers terminate): 229→| Method | Exception | Why | 231→| Worktree add/remove | `RuntimeError` | No caller inspects error content; all terminate identically | 232→| HTTP operations | `HttpError` | Generic transport failure; callers just surface the message | 236→When changing an existing gateway method's return type (e.g., converting from exception-based to discriminated union), follow this comprehensive migration pattern: 243→ - [ ] `real.py` - Return appropriate error types for failure cases 245→ - [ ] `dry_run.py` - Return appropriate success/error based on mode (4-file gateways only) 247→5. [ ] Update tests to check `isinstance(result, ErrorType)` 252→**PR #6294** (`merge_pr: bool | str` → `MergeResult | MergeError`): 256→- Updated tests to use `isinstance(result, MergeError)` 274→Some resources benefit from existence-check methods that enable Look Before You Leap validation. This pattern prevents cryptic errors when fetching non-existent resources. 282→- When error messages from `get_X()` on missing resources are unclear 305→ user_output(f"Error: Issue #{issue_number} not found") 380→6. **Unit tests** (`tests/unit/`) - Update any tests that assumed failure on missing resource 610→The worktree sub-gateway follows the same 4-file pattern with methods: `list_worktrees()`, `add_worktree()`, `move_worktree()`, `remove_worktree()`, `prune_worktrees()`, `find_worktree_for_branch()`. Note: Worktree operations use exceptions (RuntimeError), not discriminated unions. 697→**Partial removal causes type checker errors** - if you remove from abc.py but forget another implementation, the type checker will complain about missing abstract method implementations. 715→1. Run type checker (`ty`) to verify no abstract method errors 772→├── abc.py # push_to_remote() -> PushResult | PushError 774→├── fake.py # Constructor-injected errors, mutation tracking 780→- **Discriminated union returns**: Methods return `Success | Error` instead of raising 781→- **Error boundary in real.py**: `try/except RuntimeError` converts to `PushError` 782→- **Fake configuration**: `FakeGitRemoteOps(push_to_remote_error=PushError(...))` injects failures 805→- [Gateway Error Boundaries](gateway-error-boundaries.md) - Where exceptions become discriminated unions
session.assistant: Excellent. Now I have the documentation. Let me search for the actual PlanService files and CheckRunsScreen:
Perfect! Now let me read all three PlanService files and the CheckRunsScreen:

1→"""Fake plan service for testing.""" 2→ 3→from pathlib import Path 4→ 5→from erk_shared.gateway.browser.abc import BrowserLauncher 6→from erk_shared.gateway.browser.fake import FakeBrowserLauncher 7→from erk_shared.gateway.clipboard.abc import Clipboard 8→from erk_shared.gateway.clipboard.fake import FakeClipboard 9→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRCheckRun, PRReviewThread 10→from erk_shared.gateway.plan_service.abc import PlanService 11→ 12→ 13→class FakePlanService(PlanService): 14→ """Fake implementation of PlanService for testing. 15→ 16→ Returns canned data without making any API calls. 17→ """ 18→ 19→ def __init__( 20→ self, 21→ *, 22→ clipboard: Clipboard | None = None, 23→ browser: BrowserLauncher | None = None, 24→ repo_root: Path | None = None, 25→ ) -> None: 26→ """Initialize with optional canned data. 27→ 28→ Args: 29→ clipboard: Clipboard interface, defaults to FakeClipboard() 30→ browser: BrowserLauncher interface, defaults to FakeBrowserLauncher()

session.assistant:
Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile a comprehensive report for you.
---
## Report: PlanService ABC, Real, Fake, and CheckRunsScreen Pattern Analysis
Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase and documentation, here's what you need to know:
### PlanService ABC Files
#### 1. **PlanService ABC** **Path**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_service/abc.py`
**Class Definition** (lines 11-138): ```python class PlanService(ABC): """Abstract base class for plan domain operations.
Contains operations that are independent of TUI display concerns: closing plans, dispatching, fetching content, and GitHub PR data. No TUI type imports (PlanRowData, FetchTimings, PlanFilters, BranchActivity). """
@property @abstractmethod def repo_root(self) -> Path: ...
@property @abstractmethod def clipboard(self) -> Clipboard: ...
@property @abstractmethod def browser(self) -> BrowserLauncher: ...
# Abstract methods: # - close_plan(plan_id, plan_url) -> list[int] # - dispatch_to_queue(plan_id, plan_url) -> None # - fetch_plan_content(plan_id, plan_body) -> str | None # - fetch_objective_content(plan_id, plan_body) -> str | None # - get_branch_stack(branch) -> list[str] | None # - fetch_check_runs(pr_number) -> list[PRCheckRun] # - fetch_unresolved_comments(pr_number) -> list[PRReviewThread] # - fetch_ci_summaries(pr_number, *, comment_id) -> dict[str, str] ```
**Current methods**: 8 abstract methods + 3 abstract properties
---
#### 2. **RealPlanService (Production Implementation)** **Path**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_service/real.py`
**Class Definition** (lines 25-253): ```python class RealPlanService(PlanService): """Production implementation of domain plan operations."""
def __init__( self, ctx: ErkContext, *, location: GitHubRepoLocation, clipboard: Clipboard, browser: BrowserLauncher, http_client: HttpClient, ) -> None: ...
# Implements all 8 abstract methods # Key implementation patterns: # - Uses self._ctx (ErkContext) for gateway access # - Uses self._http_client for direct API calls when needed # - Uses self._location for GitHub repo info ```
**Key Pattern**: Real implementation delegates to ErkContext gateways: - `fetch_check_runs()` → calls `self._ctx.github.get_pr_check_runs()` - `fetch_unresolved_comments()` → calls `self._ctx.github.get_pr_review_threads()` - `fetch_ci_summaries()` → uses HTTP client directly with fallback path
---
#### 3. **FakePlanService (Test Implementation)** **Path**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_service/fake.py`
**Class Definition** (lines 13-200): ```python class FakePlanService(PlanService): """Fake implementation of PlanService for testing."""
def __init__( self, *, clipboard: Clipboard | None = None, browser: BrowserLauncher | None = None, repo_root: Path | None = None, ) -> None: self._clipboard = clipboard if clipboard is not None else FakeClipboard() self._browser = browser if browser is not None else FakeBrowserLauncher() self._repo_root = repo_root if repo_root is not None else Path("/fake/repo")
# Constructor-injected test data dicts self._plan_content_by_plan_id: dict[int, str] = {} self._objective_content_by_plan_id: dict[int, str] = {} self._review_threads_by_pr: dict[int, list[PRReviewThread]] = {} self._check_runs_by_pr: dict[int, list[PRCheckRun]] = {} self._ci_summaries_by_pr: dict[int, dict[str, str]] = {} self._stacks_by_branch: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
# Implements all 8 abstract methods # Pattern for test data setup: # - fetch_X() methods return from _X_by_key dicts # - set_X() methods inject test data before tests run ```
**Key Pattern**: Fake uses **constructor-injected dictionaries** for test data: - `fetch_check_runs()` → returns `self._check_runs_by_pr.get(pr_number, [])` - `set_check_runs(pr_number, check_runs)` → stores in dict for retrieval - Same pattern for all other fetch methods
---
### Where to Add `fetch_run_jobs` Method
Based on the 3-file gateway pattern documented in the architecture docs, you should add the method to **all three files simultaneously**:
#### Pattern for Adding Methods:
**abc.py** (lines ~140): ```python @abstractmethod def fetch_run_jobs(self, run_id: str) -> list[RunJob]: """Fetch job details for a specific workflow run.
Args: run_id: The GitHub workflow run ID
Returns: List of RunJob objects for the run, sorted by name """ ... ```
**real.py** (similar location): ```python def fetch_run_jobs(self, run_id: str) -> list[RunJob]: """Fetch job details for a specific workflow run.
Args: run_id: The GitHub workflow run ID
Returns: List of RunJob objects for the run, sorted by name """ return self._ctx.github.get_workflow_run_jobs(self._location.root, run_id) ```
**fake.py** (similar location): ```python def __init__(self, ...): # Add in constructor (after existing dict initializations): self._jobs_by_run: dict[str, list[RunJob]] = {}
def fetch_run_jobs(self, run_id: str) -> list[RunJob]: """Fetch job details for a specific workflow run.""" return self._jobs_by_run.get(run_id, [])
def set_run_jobs(self, run_id: str, jobs: list[RunJob]) -> None: """Set the jobs to return for a specific run.
Args: run_id: The GitHub workflow run ID jobs: List of RunJob to return """ self._jobs_by_run[run_id] = jobs ```
---
### CheckRunsScreen Structure & Pattern
**Path**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/tui/screens/check_runs_screen.py`
**Class Definition** (lines 15-231):
```python class CheckRunsScreen(ModalScreen): """Modal screen displaying failing CI check runs."""
# 1. BINDINGS for keyboard navigation (lines 18-22) BINDINGS = [ Binding("escape", "dismiss", "Close"), Binding("q", "dismiss", "Close"), Binding("space", "dismiss", "Close"), ]
# 2. DEFAULT_CSS for layout and styling (lines 24-94) DEFAULT_CSS = """ CheckRunsScreen { align: center middle; } #checks-dialog { width: 90%; max-width: 120; height: 80%; ... } #checks-loading { color: $text-muted; text-style: italic; } #checks-empty { color: $text-muted; text-style: italic; } #checks-error { color: $error; text-style: italic; } """
# 3. Constructor with dependency injection (lines 96-123) def __init__( self, *, service: PlanService, # Injected for testability pr_number: int, full_title: str, passing_count: int, total_count: int, ci_summary_comment_id: int | None, ) -> None: ...
# 4. compose() - Define UI structure (lines 125-142) def compose(self) -> ComposeResult: failing = self._total_count - self._passing_count with Vertical(id="checks-dialog"): with Vertical(id="checks-header"): yield Label(..., id="checks-title") yield Label(..., id="checks-summary", markup=False) yield Label("", id="checks-divider") with Container(id="checks-content-container"): yield Label("Loading check runs...", id="checks-loading") yield Label("Press Esc, q, or Space to close", id="checks-footer")
# 5. on_mount() - Trigger initial load (lines 144-146) def on_mount(self) -> None: self._fetch_check_runs()
# 6. Background worker - Phase 1 fetch (lines 148-159) @work(thread=True) def _fetch_check_runs(self) -> None: """Fetch failing check runs in background thread.""" check_runs: list[PRCheckRun] = [] error: str | None = None try: check_runs = self._service.fetch_check_runs(self._pr_number) except Exception as e: error = str(e) # Bridge to main thread self.app.call_from_thread(self._on_check_runs_loaded, check_runs, error)
# 7. Main thread callback - Phase 1 result handling (lines 161-195) def _on_check_runs_loaded(self, check_runs: list[PRCheckRun], error: str | None) -> None: """Handle check runs loaded - displays them, then starts Phase 2.""" container = self.query_one("#checks-content-container", Container) container.query_one("#checks-loading", Label).remove()
if error is not None: container.mount(Label(f"Error: {error}", id="checks-error")) return
if not check_runs: container.mount(Label("(No failing checks found)", id="checks-empty")) return
# Display Phase 1 results self._check_runs = check_runs container.mount( Markdown(format_check_runs(check_runs, summaries=None), id="checks-content") )
# Trigger Phase 2: optional enrichment container.mount(Label("Loading failure summaries...", id="checks-summaries-loading")) self._fetch_summaries()
# 8. Background worker - Phase 2 enrichment (lines 197-210) @work(thread=True) def _fetch_summaries(self) -> None: """Fetch CI failure summaries in background thread (optional enrichment).""" summaries: dict[str, str] = {} try: summaries = self._service.fetch_ci_summaries( self._pr_number, comment_id=self._ci_summary_comment_id ) except (RuntimeError, ValueError, KeyError): # Best-effort; silently ignore failures pass self.app.call_from_thread(self._on_summaries_loaded, summaries)
# 9. Main thread callback - Phase 2 result handling (lines 212-230) def _on_summaries_loaded(self, summaries: dict[str, str]) -> None: """Handle summaries loaded - updates display in-place.""" container = self.query_one("#checks-content-container", Container) loading = container.query("#checks-summaries-loading") for widget in loading: widget.remove()
if not summaries: return
# Update existing content (avoid remove/mount race) markdown = container.query_one("#checks-content", Markdown) markdown.update(format_check_runs(self._check_runs, summaries=summaries)) ```
---
### Key Pattern Elements for RunDetailScreen
When creating `RunDetailScreen` based on `CheckRunsScreen`, follow these 9 elements:
| Element | CheckRunsScreen Example | For RunDetailScreen | |---------|----------------------|-------------------| | 1. **Class** | `class CheckRunsScreen(ModalScreen)` | Extend `ModalScreen` (not `Screen`) | | 2. **Bindings** | escape/q/space → dismiss | Add your dismiss keys + action keys | | 3. **CSS** | `CheckRunsScreen { align: center middle; }` | Define grid layout for run details | | 4. **Constructor** | Inject `service: PlanService` | Inject run service with `run_id` | | 5. **compose()** | Build header + container + footer | Build three-part layout | | 6. **on_mount()** | Call `self._fetch_check_runs()` | Call your fetch worker | | 7. **Phase 1 worker** | `@work(thread=True) _fetch_check_runs()` | Background data load | | 8. **Phase 1 callback** | `_on_check_runs_loaded()` | Mount initial content | | 9. **Phase 2** | Optional enrichment (`_fetch_summaries()`) | Optional detail enrichment |
**Critical Patterns**: - Use **unique DOM IDs per lifecycle phase** (loading/content/error) — don't reuse `id="content"` - Always use **`call_from_thread()`** to bridge background thread to UI thread - Wrap background work in **try/except** for error boundaries - Use **`query_one()` to target unique IDs**, not `query()` which returns list - Remove loading indicators before mounting new content to avoid DOM conflicts
---
### Documentation Context
The relevant architecture documentation for this work:
1. **Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist** (`docs/learned/architecture/gateway-abc-implementation.md`): - 3-file vs 4-file patterns (PlanService uses 3-file: abc.py, real.py, fake.py) - Method addition checklist - Fake mutation tracking pattern
2. **TUI Architecture Overview** (`docs/learned/tui/architecture.md`): - PlanService lives in `erk_shared.gateway.plan_service` (not in TUI) - Describes data provider pattern and widget layer
3. **TUI Modal Screen Pattern** (`docs/learned/tui/modal-screen-pattern.md`): - 8-element checklist for new modal screens - Error boundary patterns - Async data loading with `@work(thread=True)` and `call_from_thread()`
All three files follow the **standard 3-file gateway pattern** from the architecture documentation, with clear separation between ABC contract, real implementation (using context gateways), and fake implementation (constructor-injected test data).
